reasoning: FIDE updates its official rating lists on a monthly basis, usually on the 1st of the month. The Wikipedia page 'FIDE rankings' uses hardcoded wikitables which are updated manually to reflect these monthly lists. On 28 February 2026, the page was already updated to show Praggnanandhaa's March rating of 2741 (down from 2758 in February due to his 17-point loss at Tata Steel). Therefore, his rating on Wikipedia on 2026-03-01 will be 2741. For his rating to be 1% higher, it would need to reach 2768.4. Since the FIDE list is only updated once a month, the March 8 reading will be identical to the March 1 reading (2741), resulting in a 0% change. For March 31, even if the April list were to be published early on that day, gaining 27.4 points in a single month is exceptionally rare for 2740+ rated players (requiring an unrealistic score like +6 or +7 against elite opposition). If, alternatively, the March 1 Wikipedia page still reflected his February rating of 2758, the March update to 2741 would represent a decrease, missing the target again. Given these mechanics, it is nearly impossible for the resolution criteria to be met on either date. probabilities are kept near the absolute allowed minimums, increasing slightly over time as instructed.
